title: Science history: Stephen Hawking writes a tiny paper — and turns our understanding of black holes inside out — March 1, 1974
summary: In 1974, physicist Stephen Hawking described the potential for tiny, primordial black holes that existed at the dawn of time to explode — and reshaped what we knew about these cosmic behemoths.

title: اتصالات عربية مكثفة لاحتواء التصعيد العسكري في المنطقة
summary: شهدت الاتصالات العربية، اليوم السبت، حراكا دبلوماسيا مكثفا لبحث تطورات التصعيد العسكري في المنطقة ومخاطر اتساع رقعة الصراع وانعكاساته على الأمن والاستقرار الإقليمي والدولي، إذ تلقى رئيس دولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة، محمد بن زايد آل نهيان، اتصالا هاتفيا من رئيس جمهورية مصر العربية، عبد الفتاح السيس…
body: شهدت الاتصالات العربية، اليوم السبت، حراكا دبلوماسيا مكثفا لبحث تطورات التصعيد العسكري في المنطقة ومخاطر اتساع رقعة الصراع وانعكاساته على الأمن والاستقرار الإقليمي والدولي، إذ تلقى رئيس دولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة، محمد بن زايد آل نهيان، اتصالا هاتفيا من رئيس جمهورية مصر العربية، عبد الفتاح السيسي، تناول أكمل القراءة » المقالة اتصالات عربية مكثفة لاحتواء التصعيد العسكري في المنطقة fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: NASA Is Making Big Changes to Speed Up the Artemis Program
summary: America’s journey back to the moon has run into a few missteps. NASA administrator Jared Isaacman is banking on a new approach.

title: Giant string of organic molecules on Mars may be one of the best signs of life yet
summary: A new NASA analysis concludes that it is "reasonable to hypothesize" that living things could have formed the odd organic molecules discovered on Mars.

title: 'We're starting to find a lot more weirdness': These strange animals can control their body heat
summary: Some creatures can dramatically alter their internal temperature — a strategy called heterothermy — and outlast storms, floods and predators.

title: Paleolithic humans invented an 'early predecessor to writing' at least 40,000 years ago, carved signs suggest
summary: A statistical analysis of a series of signs carved into artifacts from around 40,000 years ago suggests humans developed proto-writing in the Stone Age.

title: 16 Black-Owned Food Brands That Deserve Space In Your Kitchen
summary: Don't just save your support for February.

title: The sun just experienced its first 'spotless days' in 4 years — but we're not in the clear yet
summary: Earlier this week, the number of visible sunspots on our home star fell to zero for the first time in 1,335 days. This normally indicates a period of greatly reduced solar activity, but it's still too soon to relax, experts say.

title: NASA announces sweeping overhaul of Artemis return to moon, targeting two 2028 landings and a 2027 in-orbit docking flight
summary: A major shakeup to NASA's Artemis program will step rocket launches up to an annual basis, and discard a Boeing-designed upper stage.

title: Just in time for the total lunar eclipse, this beginner-friendly telescope is now $100 off at Amazon
summary: The Celestron StarSense Explorer DX 5-inch Schmidt-Cass is now down to $479 at Amazon, making it easy to enjoy the blood moon eclipse on March 3.

title: Science history: Carbon-14 is discovered, opening a window into past civilizations — Feb. 27, 1940
summary: Martin Kamen and Samuel Ruben's discovery of the radioactive isotope carbon-14 in 1940 helped usher in a new era of dating artifacts from past civilizations.

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.

title: 'Revolutionary': Vera C. Rubin Observatory found 800,000 objects of interest in a single night
summary: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory sent scientists nearly 1 million astronomy alerts in one night, showing off changes in the sky. Eventually, the telescope is expected to reach 7 million alerts per night.

title: Fresh look at Apollo moon rocks solves decades-old mystery about the moon's magnetic field
summary: The lunar rocks collected by Apollo astronauts suggested the moon had a strong magnetic field. A new analysis shows the opposite.

title: Babies weren't supposed to be mourned in the Roman Empire. These rare liquid-gypsum burials prove otherwise.
summary: Despite historical records saying otherwise, Roman babies were mourned at death, research into unique plaster burials from York reveals.

title: Kazakhstan plants tens of thousands of trees in giant effort to reintroduce tigers
summary: Kazakhstan planted 37,000 seedlings and cuttings in South Balkhash last year to prepare for the return of its tigers, which disappeared more than 70 years ago.

title: We now know why shoes squeak, and it involves miniature lightning bolts
summary: Harvard engineers think they've found the reason basketball shoes squeak, and it's due to pockets of friction between the rubber and the court.

title: James Webb telescope spots giant auroras rolling through Uranus' atmosphere
summary: JWST observed Uranus for nearly a full rotation, charting the planet's upper atmosphere and magnetic environment for the first time.

title: 14,000-year-old ivory tools found in Alaska hint at how Clovis ancestors first arrived in the New World
summary: Ancient artifacts unearthed in Alaska revealed migrants from Asia might have come to the Americas via an inland route, and not a coastal path.

title: Far fewer people are related to Genghis Khan than previously assumed, new genomic study suggests
summary: Some experts have suggested as many as 1 in 200 men in the world are related to Genghis Khan. But a new genomic study reveals the number is significantly lower.

title: 'Blood moon' total lunar eclipse: How to see the March 3 celestial spectacle from anywhere on Earth
summary: The final "blood moon" total lunar eclipse until 2029 is coming to North America this Tuesday (March 3). Here's how to watch it online.

title: 2,800-year-old mass grave of women and children discovered in Serbia reveals 'brutal, deliberate and efficient' violence
summary: An analysis of a mass grave found in northern Serbia is revealing new information about violence in Early Iron Age Europe.

title: Best STEM star projectors for kids in 2026
summary: These STEM star projectors make learning about constellations, planets and astronomy fun and easy for kids, plus they're ideal gifts for the little (and big) space fans.

title: Ancient China: Facts, news, features and articles about the most powerful ancient civilizations in the world
summary: Discover the latest news, features and articles about ancient China's rulers, philosophy, and the Great Wall of China.

title: Lotus shoes: Tiny footwear for Chinese women whose feet were bound as children
summary: Lotus shoes are tiny footwear associated with foot-binding, a beauty practice that lasted for at least a millennium in China.

title: Scientists find ancient black hole breaking the cosmic 'speed limit,' challenging multiple theories
summary: An ancient, fast-feeding quasar is breaking the rules of how black holes consume matter and generate galaxy-shaping jets.

title: Rare 'planetary parade' will return to the evening sky this week — but you'll have to look at exactly the right time
summary: Six planets will shine together in the evening sky on Feb. 28. Here's how to get the best view before they disappear.

title: How can deserts form next to oceans?
summary: Deserts are notoriously dry, so why do so many of them border oceans?

title: Scientists may have seen a star collapse directly into a black hole without exploding first
summary: A new study looked at how a massive star in the Andromeda Galaxy disappeared due to the formation of a black hole

title: تأثير إبستين يصل إلى "مؤتمر الديناصورات".. ماذا حدث؟
summary: قرر مؤتمر بريطاني متخصص في بحث تاريخ الديناصورات، منع حضور بعض علماء الحفريات المذكورين في ملفات جيفري إبستين، رجل الأعمال الأميركي المدان بانتهاكات جنسية.

title: في فيلم أميركي ضخم.. مشهد عن الأهرامات يثير غضب المصريين
summary: أثار مقطع من واحد من أشهر الأفلام الأميركية هذا العام، استياء عدد كبير من المصريين، بسبب معلومة تتعلق ببناء الأهرامات.
